items.title: US Commerce Department confirms harsh new AI export rules, shoots down reports over the return of Biden-era AI Diffusion rule — DoC to formalize a new approach to strategic AI accelerator export controls
items.description: The U.S. Commerce confirms work on new export regulations for AI accelerators, but implies they will not resemble the ill-fated AI Diffusion Rule.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. Department of Commerce has issued a statement confirming that the U.S. government is looking to change AI hardware export rules, but provided little by way of detail. The officials confirmed that the U.S. DoC is looking to formalize an approach under which buyers of large quantities of AI accelerators must invest in U.S. AI infrastructure to obtain their hardware.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the region"The Commerce Department is committed to promoting secure exports of the American tech stack," a statement by the U.S. DoC reads. "We successfully advanced exports through our historic Middle East agreements, and there are ongoing internal government discussions about formalizing that approach."The newly proposed export rules introduce a multi-level licensing structure tied to computing capacity. Smaller shipments of up to 1,000 Nvidia GB300 GPUs would undergo an expedited approval process; medium-scale deployments would need to secure pre-authorization from the U.S. Department of Commerce before applying for export license as well as operational transparency, disclosure of business activities, and potential on-site inspections by U.S. authorities; while planned large AI clusters set to use 200,000 of GB300 GPUs or more and operated by one entity in one country would require commitments to invest in U.S. AI infrastructure as part of the arrangement as well as intergovernmental talks with the U.S. to make national security assurances. You may like U.S. government preps sweeping export controls for Nvidia, AMD AI hardware Analyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rules U.S. posts official H200 and MI325X AI GPU export rules to China, but with plenty of caveats The terms attached to recent export licenses allowing Cerebras and Nvidia to supply hardware to the United Arab Emirates included a requirement that the Middle Eastern country invest one dollar in U.S. AI infrastructure for every dollar spent on its own domestic AI buildouts. If the same terms were to be attached to export licenses to other countries, then this would make hardware from AMD, Cerebras, Nvidia, and other suppliers of AI accelerators 2X more expensive for companies in these countries.The U.S. DoC shot down claims of a return to anything resembling the AI Diffusion Rule from the Joe Biden era: "Today there was reporting that we were returning to the AI diffusion rule," the statement reads. "We will not. It was burdensome, overreaching, and disastrous."While the AI Diffusion Rule was indeed very complicated, some approaches of the new rumored regulations would make them even more burdensome than the criticized export regime from early 2025.The AI Diffusion Rule divided countries into three tiers based on trust and risk levels. Tier 1 included the U.S. and 18 close allies like Australia, Canada, Japan, Taiwan, the U.K., and much of Western Europe, which faced minimal restrictions on AI exports. In Tier 2 countries, entities could import up to 1,700 Nvidia H100 GPUs (or equivalent) without a license, and these did not count toward national AI chip limits. Tier 3 was banned altogether.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMeanwhile, getting up to 320,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs required entities in Tier 2 countries to qualify as National Validated End Users (NVEUs) and meet strict security requirements. Unverified organizations in Tier 2 were allowed to buy up to 50,000 GPUs per country, though the limit was expandable to 100,000 if their government reached a formal agreement with the U.S. Under no circumstances did the AI Diffusion Rule mandate investments in U.S. AI infrastructure by purchasers of American AI hardware.If the new regulation is enacted, anything above 1,000 GB300 would be subject to pre-authorization before export licenses could be issued, which includes shipments to 18 allied nations. Getting 200,000 or more GB300 GPUs would essentially mandate investments in the U.S. AI buildouts. Then again, the reported rules are not final, and many of the requirements may be removed in the final versions of the proposed export regime. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 美国商务部确认严苛人工智能出口新规，驳斥拜登时期模型规则重启传闻——将明确战略人工智能加速器出口管控新方案
items.descriptionZh:
美国商务部近期证实正在酝酿修改人工智能硬件出口管制规则，其核心方向是要求大规模采购美国AI加速器的买家必须投资美国本土AI基础设施，以此作为获取硬件的条件。尽管具体实施细则尚未公布，但已披露的框架草案显示出比此前“AI扩散规则”更为复杂和严格的管控思路。

**新规核心机制：分级许可与投资挂钩**
根据披露的草案，新规将建立与算力规模直接挂钩的多级许可制度：
1.  **小规模采购（≤1,000张Nvidia GB300 GPU）**：适用快速审批流程。
2.  **中等规模采购**：需在申请出口许可证前获得美国商务部的预先授权，同时采购方需承诺运营透明度、披露商业活动，并接受美方的潜在现场检查。
3.  **大规模集群采购（≥200,000张GB300 GPU，且由单一实体在同一国家运营）**：这将成为管控重点。采购方不仅需满足上述所有条件，还必须承诺投资美国AI基础设施，并需要通过政府间对话向美国提供国家安全保证。

**关键创新与潜在成本影响**
本次规则修订最引人注目的创新点在于引入了 **“投资对等”原则**。此前美国向阿拉伯联合酋长国发放的出口许可中已出现类似条款，要求阿联酋在美投资1美元AI基础设施，才能为其国内AI建设花费1美元购买美国硬件。若此原则推广至其他国家，意味着采购美国AI加速器（涵盖AMD、Cerebras、Nvidia等供应商）的实际成本将可能**翻倍**。

**与旧规“AI扩散规则”的对比分析**
美国商务部明确否认新规是回归2025年初实施但备受批评的“AI扩散规则”，并称旧规则“繁琐、越权且具有破坏性”。两者核心差异如下：

*   **旧规（AI扩散规则）**：基于国家信任度分为三级。
    *   **第一级**：美国及18个亲密盟友（如澳、加、日、英及西欧多国），几乎无限制。
    *   **第二级**：实体可无证进口最多1,700张H100（或等效算力），不计入国家总额度。要获取多达32万张H100，需成为“国家验证最终用户”并满足严格安全要求。未经验证的组织每国最多可采购5万张（经政府间协议可扩至10万张）。**该规则从未强制要求采购方投资美国基础设施。**

*   **新规（草案）**：
    *   **适用范围扩大**：即便对第一级盟友，超过1,000张GB300的出货也需预先授权。
    *   **新增捆绑条件**：采购规模达到20万张GB300或以上时，强制要求投资美国AI基础设施。
    *   **流程更复杂**：增加了预授权、运营透明度审查及政府间安全保证谈判等多重环节。

**技术影响与应用场景展望**
若新规落地，将对全球AI产业格局产生深远影响：
1.  **供应链成本与结构**：追求超大规模AI集群（如用于训练前沿大模型）的国家或企业，将面临硬件采购成本显著上升和供应链复杂性增加的双重压力。这可能促使他们调整采购策略，或加速本土及非美替代供应链的发展。
2.  **地缘技术竞争**：规则将商业采购与地缘政治承诺（国家安全保证、基础设施投资）深度捆绑，使AI硬件贸易进一步政治化。这可能会加剧全球AI技术生态的分化。
3.  **市场准入**：对于Nvidia等美国厂商而言，其在关键区域（如文中提及的中国市场）的市场份额战略，将不得不更加复杂地平衡商业利益与合规要求，以维持其生态（如CUDA）的全球影响力。

目前，该规则仍处于讨论阶段，最终版本可能删减部分要求。但其清晰的导向表明，美国正试图通过出口管制工具，不仅限制竞争对手获取先进算力，更意图直接吸引全球资源回流，以巩固其自身在AI基础设施领域的领先地位。

items.title: Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition review:  Silent running
items.description: Asus’ GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is built with a single-minded focus on quiet operation. It takes the world’s second-fastest gaming GPU and reduces its noise levels to the absolute minimum thanks to an enormous heatsink and three cutting-edge Noctua fans. But its size, weight, high price, and polarizing design all make it a product for the Noctua faithful and quiet computing obsessives only.
items.content: Asus and Noctua have been collaborating on ultra-quiet graphics cards for some time now by pairing Noctua’s most advanced 120mm fans with massive custom heatsinks. That collaboration has continued in the Blackwell generation with the Asus RTX 5080 Noctua Edition, an absolutely ginormous air-cooled graphics card that promises no-compromises performance and temperatures alongside the lowest possible noise levels. That’s an exceedingly high bar to clear in one product. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Making air-cooled computer hardware quieter is simple enough, in theory. Improve thermal transfer by adding a vapor chamber or heat pipes to the base plate of a heatsink, increase its surface area by adding more and larger fins to the fin stack, and take advantage of the improved heat dissipation by slowing down the fans cooling said heatsink. Eventually, you get imperceptible noise levels. Easy enough, right?In practice, this recipe runs into all sorts of obstacles. Cases can only accept so large a heatsink without running into clearance issues. Sockets, slots, and PCBs can only take so much weight before they start to deform. The heatsink itself can only be so costly as part of the overall bill of materials. If you’re an engineer designing a typical graphics card for the typical PC, you have to balance all these concerns, and louder, faster-spinning fans on a smaller heatsink are typically one result of those tradeoffs.The Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition embraces an entirely different set of constraints. This graphics card is all about low noise levels, and the two companies have spared no effort or expense in making the quietest possible GPU air cooler out there, size and weight be damned.We’ve had the pleasure of reviewing some of Asus’s Noctua Edition cards in the past, and they’ve certainly provided both impressive noise levels and thermal performance. But this RTX 5080 marks the first time the duo has deployed three such fans on a Noctua Edition graphics card: in this case, NF-A12x25 G2s. As someone who got his start as a case and heatsink reviewer many moons ago, those fans immediately stand out as something different. $1,699.99 at ASUS $2,072.99 at Amazon Check Walmart (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The NF-A12x25 G2s boast a list of engineering refinements that would make an aeronautical engineer blush. Everything from the curvature of each blade to the ridges on the fan hub to the winglets at each blade tip is said to be optimized to improve the distribution and evenness of airflow with typical Noctua obsessiveness. The impellers (or rotors) themselves sit so close to the fan frame that trying to slide a sheet of printer paper between them will make the impeller move. Crazy stuff.To further refine the noise character of the card, Noctua has supplied Asus with two types of NF-A12x25 G2 fans, one of which runs slightly slower and the other of which runs slightly faster than the other. Noctua says this avoids “periodic humming or vibrations caused by beat frequencies.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The fans are paired with a custom heatsink that pairs a vapor-chamber baseplate with 11 heat pipes running through a 14.5” long fin stack, which is certainly plenty of metal. But not all of the card’s four-slot height is dedicated to fins and heat pipes. About two slots are occupied by the full-size NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We didn’t take our Noctua Edition apart because it’s a loaner, but we can see that the huge vapor chamber at the base of the heatsink covers both the GPU itself and the GDDR7 memory that rings it for a complete thermal solution. The VRM power phases and inductors are also joined to the fin stack with their own metal contact plate and thermal pads. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this is one of the largest and heaviest air-cooled graphics cards I’ve ever handled, weighing in at a whopping 5.9 lb (2.7 kg). It absolutely dwarfs the RTX 5080 Founders Edition. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)For all its focus on quiet operation, the design of the RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is inescapably polarizing. The plastic fan shroud and metal backplate are all finished in a muted brown color with a subtle sparkle that only reveals itself under direct lighting. The NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves are the brown-and-tan models that Noctua obsessives will love and the uninitiated may hate. I think it’s great, but other Tom’s Hardware staffers can’t stand it. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Noctua faithful will find plenty of subtle nods to its brand on this 5080, and these touches are all done with the same subtlety and attention to detail typical of other Noctua products. Metallic accents on the face of the card suggest wings or eyebrows, and the flow-through cutout on the backplate borrows one half of Noctua’s owl logo. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The painted stripes on the backplate that suggest stampings or embossing are so well done that they made me do a double-take to ensure they weren’t actually part of the metal. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this card will look fan-tastic in a Noctua-themed build, but many will be left wishing for a Chromax model that’s dressed entirely in black for better coordination in the average PC. Maybe a future Noctua Edition can cover both bases, but for now, brown is all you get.Let's take a look at performance, power, and thermals on the following pages.SpecsSwipe to scroll horizontallyHeader Cell - Column 0 RTX 5080 Founders EditionAsus RTX 5080 Noctua EditionGPUNvidia GB203Nvidia GB203SMs8484CUDA Cores1075210752Boost Clock (MHz)26172700VRAM TypeGDDR7GDDR7VRAM Clock17501750VRAM Capacity (GB)1616Memory Bandwidth (GB/s)960960Peak FP32 TFLOPS (Boost)56.358.1TGP (Watts)360360Power connectors1x 12V-2x61x 12V-2x6Recommended PSU (Watts)850850Dimensions (LWD)12" x 4.8" x 1.6" (30.5 x 12.2 x 4 cm)15" x 5.3" x 3.2" (38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1 cm)Weight3.6 lb (1.6 kg)5.9 lb (2.67 kg)MSRP$999.99$1,699.99MORE: Best Graphics CardsMORE: GPU Benchmarks and HierarchyMORE: All Graphics Content
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: 华硕RTX 5080猫头鹰版评测：静默运行
items.descriptionZh:
华硕（Asus）与知名散热器制造商猫头鹰（Noctua）长期合作，致力于打造极致静音的风冷显卡。双方将猫头鹰顶级的120mm风扇与特制巨型散热器结合，此前已推出多款产品。如今，这一合作延续至Blackwell世代，推出了华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition。这款风冷显卡以庞大的体积为代价，旨在实现毫不妥协的性能与温度控制，并追求可能的最低噪音水平，这无疑是一个极高的设计目标。

从理论上讲，降低风冷硬件噪音的原理相对直接：通过改进散热底座（如采用均热板或增加热管）来提升热传导效率；通过增大散热鳍片的规模和面积来增加散热表面积；进而利用更优异的散热能力来降低风扇转速，最终达到难以察觉的噪音水平。然而，在实际产品设计中，这一方案面临诸多限制：机箱兼容性、主板插槽与PCB的承重能力、以及整体物料成本。因此，常规显卡设计必须在这些因素间取得平衡，其结果往往是采用体积较小的散热器并搭配转速更高、噪音更大的风扇。

华硕GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition则完全跳出了上述常规约束。这款显卡的唯一核心目标就是低噪音，两家公司不惜一切代价和努力，致力于打造目前可能最安静的风冷GPU散热方案，而将尺寸和重量问题置于次要地位。

此次推出的RTX 5080版本标志着双方首次在猫头鹰版显卡上部署了三风扇设计，具体型号为三把NF-A12x25 G2风扇。这款风扇凝聚了猫头鹰标志性的极致工程优化，其改进清单足以令航空工程师惊叹。从每个扇叶的曲率、扇毂上的导流沟槽，到叶尖的小翼设计，一切都旨在以典型的猫头鹰式执着优化气流分布与均匀性。其转子与扇框的间隙极小，甚至难以插入一张普通打印纸，这有助于减少湍流和噪音。

为进一步优化噪音特征，猫头鹰为华硕提供了两种略有差异的NF-A12x25 G2风扇，其转速一快一慢。猫头鹰表示，这种设计可以避免因频率共振而产生的“周期性嗡嗡声或振动”。

这些风扇与一个特制散热器相连。该散热器采用均热板底座，并贯穿了11根热管，热管穿过一个长达14.5英寸（约36.8厘米）的鳍片阵列。然而，这款占据四槽位高度的显卡，并非所有空间都用于鳍片和热管，其中大约两槽的厚度被全尺寸的NF-A12x25 G2风扇本身所占据。散热器底部的巨型均热板完整覆盖了GPU核心及环绕其周围的GDDR7显存，实现了完整的核心散热。VRM供电模块和电感也通过独立的金属接触板及导热垫与鳍片阵列相连。

最终成果是史上最大、最重的风冷显卡之一，重量高达5.9磅（约2.7公斤），其体积远远超过NVIDIA的RTX 5080 Founders Edition公版卡。

尽管专注于静音，但RTX 5080 Noctua Edition的设计注定引发两极评价。其塑料风扇罩和金属背板均采用哑光棕色配色，仅在直射光下呈现细微闪亮效果。NF-A12x25 G2风扇本身也是经典的棕褐色款式，猫头鹰忠实拥趸会热爱此设计，但未接触过的用户可能难以接受。显卡正面装饰有暗示翅膀或眉毛的金属点缀，背板的通风开孔借鉴了猫头鹰品牌猫头鹰Logo的一半元素。背板上的喷绘条纹模仿冲压或浮雕效果，工艺精湛。总的来说，这款显卡在猫头鹰主题的装机中会显得极为出彩，但许多用户可能更期待未来能推出全黑色的Chromax版本以更好地搭配主流PC。

**性能规格对比与分析：**
根据提供的规格表，华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition在核心规格（如SM数量、CUDA核心数、显存类型、容量、带宽及TGP功耗）上与公版RTX 5080 Founders Edition完全一致。其主要提升和差异体现在：
1.  **频率与性能**：其Boost时钟频率从公版的2617MHz提升至2700MHz，这使得其峰值FP32算力（Boost）达到58.1 TFLOPS，略高于公版的56.3 TFLOPS。
2.  **尺寸与重量**：这是最显著的差异。其尺寸（38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1 厘米）远大于公版（30.5 x 12.2 x 4 厘米），重量（2.67公斤）更是比公版（1.6公斤）重了约67%。
3.  **价格**：其建议零售价高达1699.99美元，相比公版卡的999.99美元有大幅溢价。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition代表了风冷显卡设计的一个极端方向。它证明了通过不计体积和成本的极致堆料，确实能在不牺牲性能的前提下，将高功耗GPU的噪音控制在极低水平。这为那些对噪音极其敏感的用户（如音频制作、静音办公环境、追求极致安静体验的发烧友）提供了顶级风冷解决方案。它重新定义了“旗舰风冷”的标准，即不再仅仅追求绝对散热效能，而是将“静音下的效能”作为更高维度的目标。然而，其庞大的体积、惊人的重量和昂贵的售价也决定了它只能是面向小众高端市场的产品，对机箱兼容性和主板承重提出了严峻挑战。它更像是一个展示双方工程理念和技术实力的“光环产品”，其设计思路可能会影响未来高端风冷显卡在平衡噪音与效能时对散热模组规模的重新考量。

items.title: Arc Raiders was accidentally recording Discord conversations into an unencrypted local game file — vulnerability in SDK could log messages and credentials in plaintext
items.description: A vulnerability in Discord's SDK allowed Arc Raiders to unintentionally log private DMs and user account credentials to the game's log files. Embark Studios has since hotfixed the problem.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A computer engineer has discovered a serious vulnerability within Discord's SDK that allows games to store Discord DMs between players in game logs without any security measures. System engineer Timothy Meadows published a blog post, revealing an incident where Arc Raiders was storing DMs between two gamers in plaintext to a local log file. Thankfully, at the time of writing, the problem has since been hotfixed by Embark Studios.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCTimothy discovered that Arc Raiders' Discord SDK was using a completely unencrypted bearer token and logs "all events" including any private conversations to the user's local drive without any encryption. A bearer token stores the user's Discord credentials, and anyone who gets this token has full access to the Discord user's account, including private DMs, friends list, and account settings.This is made worse by the fact that if Arc Raiders crashes and the user sends log files to Embark Studios (the game's development team), the company's employees will have that user's full account credentials and any DMs that were sent to the log files. You may like Steam client allegedly continues sharing your status with your friends even if you set it ‘Offline,’ report claims Security researcher says AMD auto-updater downloads software insecurely, enabling remote code execution User accidentally gains control of over 6,700 robot vacuums while tinkering with their own device Arc Raider uses the Discord SDK to show your Discord friends list in-game and invite Discord friends to the game. For this limited functionality, Timothy states the game only requires a "limited OAuth scope for game activity display." This would solve the issue and stop Arc Raiders from recording DMs to log files and storing a user's full account credentials to the game's log files as well. Some engineers who've inspected Discord's API say the issue lies solely with Discord, however.I dug into the ARC Raiders Discord token leak issue; this might not be ARC Raiders or Embark's fault. Discord's new Social SDK has a logging hook you can override, and as far as I can tell Discord is failing to scrub log events of sensitive information. API: discord.com/developers/d...— @eidolon.photon.institute (@eidolon.photon.institute.bsky.social) 2026-03-05T19:48:37.434ZThankfully, Embark Studios has since patched the issue with a hotfix. The game company assured users that no private or personal data was sent outside of gamers' PCs, and the company itself has not reviewed or kept any personal information that might have been sent to them. Embark Studios has completely disabled Discord's SDK and is conducting an audit to ensure that there are no other problems with the SDK.This isn't the first time Discord has to deal with security issues. The social app was hacked by a ransomware group late last year, demanding $3.5 million from Discord's developers, and allegedly stole 70,000 government ID photos. items.titleZh: 《Arc Raiders》意外将Discord对话存入未加密本地游戏文件——SDK漏洞可能以明文记录消息与登录凭证
items.descriptionZh:
一名计算机工程师发现了Discord SDK中的一个严重安全漏洞，该漏洞允许游戏在没有任何安全措施的情况下，将玩家之间的Discord私聊信息存储于游戏日志中。系统工程师Timothy Meadows发表了一篇博客文章，披露了游戏《Arc Raiders》将两名玩家之间的私信以明文形式记录到本地日志文件的事件。所幸，在本文撰写时，游戏开发商Embark Studios已通过热修复紧急修补了该问题。

该漏洞的核心技术问题在于，《Arc Raiders》游戏使用的Discord SDK采用了一种完全未加密的承载令牌（bearer token），并将“所有事件”——包括任何私人对话——在不经任何加密的情况下记录到用户的本地硬盘。承载令牌存储了用户的Discord凭证，任何获得此令牌的人都将拥有该Discord账户的完全访问权限，包括查看私信、好友列表和账户设置。

更严重的安全隐患在于，如果《Arc Raiders》游戏崩溃，用户将日志文件发送给开发商Embark Studios，该公司的员工便能获得该用户的完整账户凭证以及记录在日志中的所有私信内容。

《Arc Raiders》游戏使用Discord SDK是为了在游戏中显示Discord好友列表并邀请好友加入游戏。Timothy指出，仅为实现此有限功能，游戏只需要一个“用于显示游戏活动的有限OAuth授权范围”。若正确配置，本可避免该问题，防止游戏将私信记录到日志文件，也避免将用户的完整账户凭证存入游戏日志。

然而，一些检查过Discord API的工程师认为，问题根源可能在于Discord本身。有分析指出，Discord的新社交SDK包含一个可供覆盖的日志钩子（logging hook），而Discord未能从日志事件中清除敏感信息。

值得庆幸的是，Embark Studios已通过热修复修补了此漏洞。该公司向用户保证，没有私人或个人数据被发送到玩家电脑之外，公司自身也未查看或保留任何可能发送给他们的个人信息。目前，Embark Studios已完全禁用了Discord SDK，并正在进行审计，以确保SDK没有其他问题。

这并非Discord首次面临安全问题。去年年底，该社交应用曾遭勒索软件组织攻击，攻击者向Discord开发者索要350万美元，并据称窃取了7万张政府身份证照片。

items.title: US gov't preps sweeping export controls for Nvidia, AMD AI hardware — worldwide licensing system would give Trump admin broad authority to block global sales
items.description: The Trump administration is working on sweeping export regulations over AI hardware that will make large AI clusters a topic of intergovernmental talks.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. government is drafting sweeping new export regulations that would give it authority to approve nearly all global shipments of advanced AI accelerators made by American companies, including AMD and Nvidia, reports Bloomberg. The rules would expand existing country-based restrictions into a worldwide licensing system and will give Trump's administration power to allow or decline large-scale AI infrastructure buildouts. The rule turns out to be considerably stricter than the highly-criticized AI Diffusion Rule from the Biden era. Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe newly proposed export regime establishes a tiered licensing system based on computing scale: small-scale batches would pass through a simplified review, mid-scale deployments would require 'preclearance before seeking export licenses' from the U.S. Department of Commerce, whereas large-scale AI clusters would require governments of host countries to negotiate the deal with the U.S. government and investments in the U.S. AI infrastructure as part of the deal.Shipments involving up to 1,000 Nvidia GB300 GPUs would pass through a simplified review and may qualify for limited exemptions.Larger installations would require pre-authorization before export licenses could be issued. These will introduce compliance obligations that may include operational transparency, disclosure of business activities, and potential on-site inspections by U.S. authorities.Clusters powered by 200,000 GB300 GPUs operated by a single company within one country — such as those currently deployed by AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, Open AI, or xAI — would trigger direct intergovernmental arrangements. In these cases, approvals would depend on national-security assurances as well as commitments to invest in American AI infrastructure.For now, companies like AMD, Cerebras, and Nvidia cannot ship high-performance AI processors to select countries, such as China, Iran, North Korea, and Russia, just to name a few. Yet, shipping large-scale batches of AI accelerators to Middle Eastern countries requires direct intergovernmental arrangements and investments in the American AI infrastructure. If the newly proposed regulation becomes a law, then such arrangements would become much more common particularly with allied nations in Europe. You may like US Commerce Department confirms harsh new AI export rules, shoots down reports over the return of Biden-era AI Diffusion rule Analyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rules U.S. posts official H200 and MI325X AI GPU export rules to China, but with plenty of caveats The proposed policy is by no means a total export ban, but an extremely powerful tool that lets the U.S. government influence how global AI infrastructure develops. If the Trump administration does not like the fact that a UK or France-based company deploys a cluster of over 200,000 GB300 GPUs or equivalent, it can deny appropriate export licenses if appropriate governments and companies do not meet their requirements.If the proposed policy is enacted, for companies like AMD or Nvidia, which ship their products globally, everything will depend on how quickly licenses are granted and what conditions are attached. Fast approvals with light restrictions would let most small projects move forward, though with more paperwork. However, for larger installations, tougher requirements would delay construction and make operating large data centers outside the U.S. considerably more complicated. Meanwhile, building clusters comparable to those currently operated by AWS, Google, Oracle, Microsoft, or xAI outside of the U.S. would face extreme complications, which would lower their economic feasibility. items.titleZh: 美政府拟对英伟达、AMD人工智能硬件实施全面出口管制——全球许可制度将赋予特朗普政府阻止全球销售的广泛权力
items.descriptionZh:
根据彭博社报道，美国政府正在起草一套全面而严厉的新出口管制规则草案。该草案将赋予美国政府审批几乎所有由美国公司（包括AMD和英伟达）生产的先进AI加速器全球发货的权力。此举旨在将现有的、主要针对特定国家（如中国）的出口限制，扩展为一个覆盖全球的、基于计算规模的**分级许可制度**，从而使特朗普政府获得批准或否决全球范围内大规模AI基础设施建设的强大影响力。报道指出，这一拟议规则比此前备受批评的拜登时代的“AI扩散规则”**更为严格**。

### **新规核心技术原理与创新点**
拟议规则的核心创新在于**以“计算规模”作为管制门槛**，建立了一个三层级的全球审查体系：
1.  **小规模部署**：涉及**最多1000个英伟达GB300 GPU（或性能等效产品）** 的发货将通过简化审查，并可能获得有限的豁免。
2.  **中规模部署**：规模超过上述门槛的部署，需要在美国商务部颁发出口许可证之前，获得“预批准”。
3.  **大规模集群**：对于在一个国家内由单一公司运营、规模达到**约20万个GB300 GPU** 的巨型AI集群（例如当前由AWS、微软、OpenAI或xAI部署的级别），将触发**政府间直接协议**。此类交易的批准，将取决于东道国政府提供国家安全保证，并承诺**投资美国的AI基础设施**作为交易的一部分。

### **性能参数、对比数据与合规义务**
*   **量化阈值**：规则以GPU数量（GB300为基准）明确划定了监管边界。例如，1000个GPU是简化审查与预批准的分界线；20万个GPU则是启动政府间谈判的阈值。
*   **对比现有政策**：目前，AMD、Cerebras、英伟达等公司的高性能AI处理器已被禁止向中国、伊朗、俄罗斯等特定国家发货。而新规将**大型发货的管制范围从“特定国家”扩展到“全球所有地区”**，包括欧洲等盟友。
*   **新增合规负担**：对于中大型规模的出口，企业将面临包括运营透明度、业务活动披露以及可能接受美国当局现场检查在内的合规义务。

### **技术影响与应用场景分析**
这项政策若最终立法，将对全球AI产业格局产生深远影响：
1.  **对美国AI芯片制造商的影响**：对于AMD、英伟达等全球性企业，其产品发货的节奏将完全取决于许可证的审批速度和附加条件。快速、宽松的审批能让中小型项目在更多文书工作下推进，但大型项目的建设将被延迟，在海外运营超大规模数据中心的复杂性将急剧增加。
2.  **对全球云服务商及AI公司的影响**：试图在美国以外（如英国、法国）建设堪比当前AWS、谷歌、微软规模AI集群的经济可行性将大幅降低。美国政府可以借此工具，直接塑造全球AI基础设施的发展轨迹，若其对某海外大型项目不满，可通过拒发许可证进行干预。
3.  **对地缘政治与产业生态的影响**：规则将促使希望获得最先进AI算力的国家或公司，通过承诺投资美国本土AI基础设施来换取技术准入。这不仅能巩固美国在AI硬件领域的领导地位，还可能实质性引导全球资本流入美国的AI基建领域。同时，这也可能刺激其他地区加速研发替代性AI芯片解决方案。

总而言之，该拟议政策并非全面的出口禁令，而是一个**极其强大的监管工具**，使美国政府能够深度介入并影响全球AI基础设施的布局与发展，标志着对先进AI算力的地缘政治管控达到了新的高度。

items.title: As Frank Yeary retires from Intel, the company picks an engineer to chair its board — Intel Foundry governance issues to be resolved, and looking back at the Yeary years
items.description: Intel’s board of directors is getting a new independent chair, effective May 13, following the company’s Annual Stockholders’ Meeting.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel’s board of directors is getting a new independent chair effective May 13, after the company’s Annual Stockholders’ Meeting, the company announced earlier this week. Dr. Craig H. Barratt, a semiconductor engineer by training — not to be confused with former CEO Craig R. Barrett — will assume the role, succeeding finance veteran Frank D. Yeary, who has served on the board since 2009, and as chair since 2023.Intel made the announcement on March 3, clarifying that Yeary will not stand for reelection in May. It's the most consequential governance change at Intel since the board’s forced exit of former CEO Pat Gelsinger in late 2024. It also consolidates authority around Barratt, who will lead the board’s focus on scaling U.S. R&D and manufacturing, and CEO Lip-Bu Tan, who has publicly supported Intel Foundry since taking the role.Who is Craig H. Barratt?Barratt, 63, holds bachelor's degrees in pure mathematics, physics, and electrical engineering from the University of Sydney, as well as a master's and doctorate in electrical engineering from Stanford. You may like Intel Chairman Frank Yeary retires, Craig Barrat to become the new chairman of the Board of Directors Intel Foundry boss leaves for Qualcomm Intel appoints Trump economic advisor as head of government affairs He joined Atheros Communications in 2002 as vice president of technology, was appointed president and CEO in 2003, and led the company for eight years. Atheros was the dominant supplier of Wi-Fi chipsets during the early 802.11 boom, shipping silicon into PCs, consumer routers, smartphones, and networking gear. Under Barratt, the company went public in 2004 and was acquired by Qualcomm in 2011 for roughly $3.1 billion. He then served as president of Qualcomm Atheros through 2013, overseeing the combined non-cellular connectivity and networking silicon business.After leaving Qualcomm, Barratt ran Google Access and Energy as senior vice president from 2012 to 2016, overseeing Google Fiber and related internet infrastructure projects. He then took over as CEO of Barefoot Networks, a startup building programmable Ethernet switching ASICs, from 2017 until Intel acquired the company in 2019. Post-acquisition, he became senior vice president and general manager of Intel's Ethernet, silicon photonics, and networking businesses, a role he held through 2020. He joined Intel’s board as an independent director in November 2025.None of that is related to CPU or process technology, though; Barratt has never run a foundry or managed a node transition. What he has done is build and sell semiconductor businesses, work inside Intel as an executive, and, most recently, sit on the board of a company whose products are deeply embedded in the AI infrastructure supply chain — something Intel is trying to break into with its Gaudi accelerators and Intel Foundry.One of Intel’s ‘most consequential’ periods (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Yeary, by contrast, came from investment banking and financial advisory, having spent more than two decades in those fields before joining Intel's board in 2009. His tenure as chair, beginning in 2023, coincided with the most financially damaging stretch in the company's recent history, with losses exceeding $16.6 billion in Q3 2024 alone, a market capitalization that fell dramatically, and a floundering foundry operation that has not yet approached profitability.Yeary took consequential actions during that period and is understood to have orchestrated Gelsinger's removal. He then brought in Lip-Bu Tan and shepherded an intentional board refresh that has added four independent directors with technology and operational backgrounds since 2024.“Frank led the effort to bring me in as the company’s CEO, encouraged disciplined board oversight, and reinforced strong board governance. With his and the board’s support, I have been empowered to take decisive actions to strengthen our financial foundation, advance our process roadmap, and position the company for long-term competitiveness,” said Tan in an official press release announcing Yeary’s retirement.But some industry analysts have reported that Yeary and Tan were not aligned on keeping Intel Foundry. In a post to X on August 7, Citrini Research analyst Jukan cited insiders who claimed that Yeary drafted a plan to spin off Intel Foundry as an independent entity, bring in minority investment from companies including Nvidia and Amazon, and effectively step back from contract manufacturing as a core business. What to read next Eric Demers leaves for Intel after 14 years at Qualcomm Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' President Trump, Sec. Lutnick praise Intel after the launch of Panther Lake chips "With a stronger balance sheet, meaningful progress across our roadmap — including Intel 18A and 14A — and a clear path forward under Lip-Bu, this is the appropriate time for me to step down as chair and from the board and transition leadership to a new independent chair,” said Yeary.Almost at the start of his term, CEO Tan and Board Chairman Frank Yeary were split over whether to continue Intel’s semiconductor manufacturing and foundry business or to exit it.Chairman Yeary had drafted a plan to spin off the loss-making foundry business and invite minority… pic.twitter.com/rRQjKJzSyFAugust 8, 2025Tan opposed it, arguing that the foundry is both “essential to Intel’s success and necessary for the U.S. to secure” a domestic alternative to TSMC. None of this has been acknowledged by Intel, however, so it should be read with appropriate caution.What’s confirmed is that Yeary is leaving, and the new chair has publicly stated that his focus will be on supporting “rigorous execution” in investing and scaling U.S. R&D and manufacturing.Proving 18A at scale The 288-core Intel Clearwater Forest Xeon CPU, based on the company's 18A process. (Image credit: Intel)Tan has staked Intel’s recovery on first proving that 18A can work at scale, and second, convincing external customers to commit to Intel Foundry for production. Neither objective is achievable if the board retains any serious appetite for spinning out or selling the Intel Foundry business, which posted an operating loss of $13.4 billion in 2024.Intel launched Panther Lake (Core Ultra Series 3) on 18A at CES in January, with consumer systems shipping later that month, making it the first commercial platform built on the node and the most advanced process ever manufactured in the United States, using RibbonFET gate-all-around transistors and PowerVia backside power delivery.Yields are sufficient to support Panther Lake shipments, but Intel CFO David Zinsner said in October that they are not yet high enough to deliver normal profit margins, with industry-standard yield results not expected until 2027. It’s understood that Intel doesn’t plan to add significant 18A capacity in 2026 beyond current commitments.In Barratt, Intel will gain a chair who has publicly committed to scaling U.S. manufacturing, who operates inside a semiconductor ecosystem that depends on foundry diversity, and who has worked inside Intel's own infrastructure business. That’ll obviously matter to potential foundry customers doing long-term planning who need confidence that Intel Foundry will still exist and be resourced in five years."The company has taken significant steps to strengthen its financial position, advance its technology and product roadmap, and enhance operational discipline," said Barratt. "The board thanks Frank for his leadership and for helping position Intel for this next phase."Intel's board will also shrink from 12 to 11 members following the May meeting, as Yeary's seat will not be filled. Given that four new directors with technology operating backgrounds have joined the board since 2024, its overall composition is moving in a consistent direction away from purely financial oversight and towards technical expertise.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 弗兰克·耶里从英特尔退休，公司任命工程师担任董事会主席——英特尔代工部门治理问题待解，回顾耶里任期
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔公司近日宣布，其董事会将迎来新任独立主席。自5月13日年度股东大会后，半导体工程师出身的克雷格·H·巴雷特博士（Dr. Craig H. Barratt）将接替金融背景深厚的弗兰克·D·耶里（Frank D. Yeary），后者自2009年加入董事会并于2023年担任主席，现已决定不寻求连任。此次人事变动是自2024年底董事会强制前首席执行官帕特·盖尔辛格（Pat Gelsinger）离职以来，英特尔最为重大的治理结构调整，旨在强化公司在技术与制造领域的战略聚焦，特别是在当前关键转型期。

巴雷特现年63岁，拥有悉尼大学纯数学、物理学和电气工程学士学位，以及斯坦福大学电气工程硕士和博士学位。他的职业生涯始于2002年加入Atheros通信公司，2003年出任总裁兼CEO，领导这家在早期802.11 Wi-Fi浪潮中主导芯片组市场的企业直至2011年被高通以约31亿美元收购。此后，他担任高通Atheros总裁至2013年，负责非蜂窝连接与网络芯片业务。2012年至2016年，巴雷特在谷歌担任高级副总裁，主管Google Fiber及互联网基础设施项目；2017年至2019年，他担任可编程以太网交换芯片初创企业Barefoot Networks的CEO，该公司后被英特尔收购，他随之出任英特尔以太网、硅光子和网络业务高级副总裁兼总经理至2020年。巴雷特于2025年11月以独立董事身份加入英特尔董事会。尽管他未曾直接管理晶圆厂或制程节点转型，但其背景涵盖半导体企业构建、出售以及英特尔内部运营经验，且近期在涉及AI基础设施供应链的董事会任职，这与英特尔正通过Gaudi加速器和英特尔代工（Intel Foundry）进军该领域的战略相契合。

相比之下，耶里来自投资银行和金融咨询领域，其主席任期恰逢英特尔近年财务最严峻阶段：2024年第三季度单季亏损超过166亿美元，市值大幅缩水，代工业务持续亏损且未实现盈利。耶里在此期间采取了关键行动，包括主导盖尔辛格的离职，并引入现任CEO陈立武（Lip-Bu Tan）。然而，行业分析指出，耶里与陈立武在代工业务战略上存在分歧。据传耶里曾起草计划，意图分拆亏损的代工业务为独立实体，引入英伟达、亚马逊等少数投资，从而收缩合同制造核心业务；但陈立武强烈反对，认为代工业务对英特尔成功及美国构建台积电替代方案至关重要。英特尔官方未证实此分歧，但耶里的离任与新主席的任命被视为战略方向统一的信号。

技术层面，英特尔的复苏依赖于18A制程节点的规模化验证及外部客户对英特尔代工的采纳。18A节点采用RibbonFET全环绕栅极晶体管和PowerVia背面供电技术，是迄今美国本土最先进的制程。2025年1月CES上发布的Panther Lake（酷睿Ultra系列3）处理器基于18A生产，已实现商用出货。然而，当前良率虽支持出货，却未达正常利润水平，英特尔CFO大卫·津斯纳（David Zinsner）表示行业标准良率需至2027年方能实现；公司2026年暂无大规模扩充18A产能的计划。财务数据显示，英特尔代工业务2024年运营亏损达134亿美元，凸显转型挑战。

巴雷特就任后公开强调，将聚焦支持“严谨执行”美国研发与制造的投入与扩张。他的背景被认为能增强潜在代工客户的长期信心，因其熟悉半导体生态对代工多元化的需求，且曾领导英特尔网络业务。同时，董事会规模将从12人缩减至11人，且自2024年以来新增的四名独立董事均具技术运营背景，标志着董事会组成正从纯财务监督转向技术专业主导，以匹配公司以技术驱动复兴的战略路径。

整体而言，此次人事更迭标志着英特尔在财务承压与技术攻坚的双重挑战下，进一步将治理权重倾斜至技术与制造领域，试图通过稳定代工业务、推进先进制程，重塑在全球半导体竞争中的地位。巴雷特的任命或有助于强化内部战略协同，加速18A节点成熟与代工业务拓展，但其成效仍需观察技术落地与市场接纳的实际情况。

items.title: China's top chip execs claim ASML alternative 'small, fragmented, and weak' — Chinese industry titans call for national effort to invest in advanced chipmaking tools
items.description: China's most senior semiconductor executives issued a public call this week for a consolidated national effort to build a domestic alternative to Dutch lithography giant ASML.

Subscribe to our newsletter China's most senior semiconductor executives issued a public call this week for a consolidated national effort to build a domestic alternative to Dutch lithography giant ASML, warning that the country's chip equipment industry remains too "small, fragmented, and weak" to overcome U.S. export restrictions on its own.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingIn remarks co-authored by SMIC co-founder Wang Yangyuan alongside leaders of memory giant YMTC, chip equipment maker Naura, and EDA software developer Empyrean, three specific areas where U.S. export controls have choked China's semiconductor ambitions were identified: electronic design automation software, silicon wafers, and manufacturing equipment — particularly extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography, which enables the sub-7nm chip production that China currently cannot replicate.As reported by the South China Morning Post, the authors urged the industry to "abandon illusions and prepare for struggle," and argued that fragmented public funding was dispersing resources across too many competing efforts without producing results. You may like Chinese fabs are reportedly upgrading older ASML DUV lithography chipmaking machines U.S. lawmakers demand sales ban on chipmaking tools to China China's reverse-engineered Frankenstein EUV chipmaking tool hasn't produced a single chip China is currently drafting its 15th Five-Year Plan, which is set to be revealed to the National People’s Congress within the next week and covers 2026 to 2030. It’s widely expected that this iteration of the plan will prioritize lithography breakthroughs and EDA tool development as national targets. Big Fund III, a state-backed vehicle with roughly $47.5 billion earmarked for semiconductors, has already redirected fresh capital toward lithography and EDA as substitutes for ASML and Synopsys tools, respectively.The sheer candor about fragmentation from this collection of executives is difficult to ignore, however, given just how often we see big, bold tech claims coming from Chinese media, but it aligns with an increasingly obvious challenge. China's most advanced domestically produced DUV lithography system, from Yuliangsheng, is technically comparable to ASML's Twinscan NXT:1950i — a machine ASML originally designed for 32nm-class processes back in 2008.Even if SMIC manages to integrate that tool into a 28nm process by 2027, reaching sub-10nm would require redesigned scanners and several additional years of development. A prototype EUV machine has reportedly been completed in a Shenzhen lab, but EUV's commercial viability requires solving yield challenges that took ASML nearly two decades to overcome after its own prototype.That’s all before we’ve even considered ASML’s accumulated know-how as, in Yangyuan’s words, “merely the integrator.” The company’s EUV dominance rests on a supply chain of more than 5,000 subcontractors, along with decades of high-volume manufacturing data. No amount of reverse engineering can quickly replicate that. While it’s true that Chinese firms have made real gains in adjacent equipment categories — Naura, for example, is one of the world’s top ten semi equipment vendors by revenue — lithography remains well out of reach.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 中国芯片高层称ASML替代技术"小而散、弱"——产业巨头呼吁举全国之力投资先进芯片制造工具
items.descriptionZh:
近日，中国半导体行业的数位顶尖高管联合发表公开呼吁，主张整合全国力量以构建能够替代荷兰光刻巨头ASML的本土化解决方案。他们警告称，中国的芯片设备产业目前仍过于“规模小、分散且薄弱”，难以单独突破美国的出口管制。

这篇由中芯国际联合创始人王阳元、存储巨头长江存储、芯片设备制造商北方华创以及EDA软件开发商芯愿景的领导人共同署名的文章，明确指出了美国出口管制严重制约中国半导体雄心的三大关键领域：电子设计自动化（EDA）软件、硅片以及制造设备——尤其是用于7纳米以下芯片生产的极紫外（EUV）光刻机，而中国目前尚无法复制此类技术。

据《南华早报》报道，这些行业领袖敦促产业界“放弃幻想，准备斗争”，并指出当前分散的公共资金将资源分散到了太多相互竞争的项目中，却未能产出实质性成果。

**技术差距与核心挑战**

*   **光刻机代差**：中国目前最先进的国产深紫外（DUV）光刻系统来自玉亮生，其技术水平大致相当于ASML于2008年设计的、用于32纳米级工艺的Twinscan NXT:1950i设备。分析指出，即使中芯国际成功将该设备整合并应用于28纳米制程（预计2027年），要实现10纳米以下工艺，仍需重新设计扫描仪并投入数年时间进行研发。
*   **EUV鸿沟**：据报道，中国已在深圳实验室完成了一台EUV光刻原型机。然而，EUV要具备商业可行性，必须解决其良率挑战，而ASML从其原型机到克服这些挑战花费了将近二十年时间。王阳元指出，ASML“仅仅是集成商”，但其EUV主导地位建立在超过5000家供应商的供应链以及数十年的大规模制造数据积累之上。这种深厚的知识体系和生态系统，是任何逆向工程都无法在短期内复制的。
*   **产业链现状**：尽管中国企业在部分周边设备领域取得了切实进展（例如北方华创已跻身全球半导体设备供应商营收前十），但光刻机——尤其是EUV光刻机——依然是遥不可及的目标。行业整体仍面临“小、散、弱”的困境。

**政策背景与产业动向**

当前，中国正在制定第十五个五年规划（“十五五”规划，涵盖2026-2030年），预计将于近期提交全国人民代表大会。业界普遍预期，该规划将把光刻技术突破和EDA工具开发列为国家优先目标。

与此同时，规模约475亿美元的国家集成电路产业投资基金三期（“大基金三期”）已经启动，据信已将新的资金导向光刻和EDA领域，旨在分别替代ASML和新思科技（Synopsys）的工具。

**分析与展望**

此次高管们对产业分散化问题的坦率承认值得关注，这与通常来自中国媒体的大胆技术声明形成对比，却也印证了一个日益明显的挑战。中国在半导体自主化道路上面临的是需要长期投入、系统化构建的全产业链能力，而非单一设备的突破。在可预见的未来，追赶ASML在尖端光刻领域积累的领先优势，将是一项极其艰巨的任务。中国的努力方向正从广泛尝试转向在关键瓶颈领域进行更有针对性的资源整合与攻坚。

items.title: ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI, shortages — Lisa Su says business exceeded expectations while Intel is looking at long-term agreements with potential customers
items.description: Both AMD and Intel reports increased demand for CPUs, driven by AI agents' need for general computing power.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 21 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Both AMD and Intel said during the 2026 Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference that demand for CPUs is seeing an uptick due to artificial intelligence. Intel CFO David Zinsner said during his question and answer (via Investing.com) that “the CPU has become cool again this year,” especially as AI agents need CPUs to orchestrate the computationally-heavy tasks that the GPUs and NPUs will execute. It has even started seeing customers who are looking at long-term agreements, ensuring that they’ll have a continuous supply of these chips needed to expand their operations. On the other hand, AMD CEO Lisa Su said during the same conference, “You know, we’re seeing a significant CPU demand, frankly, as a result of the inference demand picking up.” She also added later that “the CPU portion of the business has actually far exceeded my expectations in terms of demand.”Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe AI boom has been driving a shortage in various components since ChatGPT showed some potential in late 2022. It first began with GPUs, especially as data centers and hyperscalers have been buying these components en masse to build massive servers with hundreds of thousands of GPUs. As graphics card supply started to normalize around the middle of 2025, experts and analysts have started warning about memory and storage chip shortages due to massive demand for high-bandwidth memory and enterprise-grade storage in AI data centers.We felt the full swing of this crisis in the fourth quarter of last year, with pricing for RAM modules and SSDs continuing to rise through February 2026. What makes this worse than the GPU shortage is that it has a much wider impact. While the GPUs that were in short supply were mostly limited to desktop PCs and gaming laptops with a discrete graphics card, virtually every modern digital device — from consumer devices like smart TVs and smartphones to automobiles and industrial-grade equipment — needs memory and storage. And consumer-grade memory and storage is fighting for the same wafer space that enterprise-grade memory and storage could occupy, usually with a far higher price tag. You may like Intel, AMD server CPUs reportedly suffering from supply shortages in China AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 'We can't completely vacate the client market' says Intel amid wafer supply shortages As AI advancements move forward from large language models and chatbots to agents that can observe, reason, plan, act, and learn independently, data centers require more multi-processor computing power — that means combining CPUs, GPUs, NPUs, and more — to support the entire agentic AI workflow. China is starting to see this spike in demand, with both Team Blue and Team Red reporting supply shortages for server CPUs in the region. We’re also seeing a spike in demand for high-end Mac Studios and Mac minis, especially as we see more people build their own local AI agents with the rise in popularity of the open-source Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw.AMD and Intel are presumably talking about data center demand for their CPUs; consumer systems aren't equipped to handle the massive memory demands of agentic AI. If there is a shortage, however, it could trickle down to the consumer market, assuming the demand keeps pace.Over the past several generations, AMD and Intel have converged their data center and consumer offerings, allowing them to maximize yields by leveraging the same microarchitecture across both client and enterprise. Some of that silicon won't be useful in the data center, so the consumer market won't evaporate. But it could put downward pressure on supply if the focus shifts toward the data center, as we've seen with RAM and SSDs.Unlike Nvidia, which has seen exponential increases in revenue in its data center business, both AMD and Intel still see about half of their total revenue each quarter from the consumer market. It's still an important market, so although demand from data centers may increase, it shouldn't come at the cost of the consumer market, at least entirely.Hopefully, both Intel and AMD can keep up with the future demand, so as not to exacerbate the worsening situation of the computer industry. Otherwise, some are already predicting the end of the entry-level PC by 2028 if things continue as they are.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: CPU市场因智能体AI激增与缺货再度大热，英特尔与AMD需求飙升——苏姿丰称业务超预期，英特尔寻求与潜在客户签订长期协议
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年摩根斯坦利技术、媒体与电信大会上，AMD和英特尔均指出，由于人工智能的推动，CPU需求正在显著回升。英特尔首席财务官David Zinsner表示，“今年CPU再次变得酷炫起来”，因为AI智能体需要CPU来协调和管理由GPU和NPU执行的计算密集型任务。公司甚至开始接到寻求长期供货协议的客户，以确保其业务扩张所需的芯片持续供应。AMD首席执行官苏姿丰在同一会议上也表示，“我们看到CPU需求显著增长，坦率地说，这源于推理需求的提升”，并补充说“CPU业务部分的需求实际上远超我的预期”。

这一现象的背景是自2022年底ChatGPT展现潜力以来，AI热潮持续引发各类组件短缺。短缺最初集中在GPU，因为数据中心和超大规模云服务商大规模采购以构建配备数十万GPU的巨型服务器。随着显卡供应在2025年年中左右开始正常化，专家和分析师转而警告由于AI数据中心对高带宽内存和企业级存储的庞大需求，可能导致内存和存储芯片短缺。这场危机在去年第四季度全面爆发，RAM模块和SSD的价格持续上涨至2026年2月。与GPU短缺相比，内存和存储短缺的影响范围更广，因为从智能电视、智能手机到汽车和工业设备，几乎所有现代数字设备都需要内存和存储，且消费级产品与企业级产品在晶圆产能上存在竞争。

随着AI技术从大型语言模型和聊天机器人，演进到能够观察、推理、规划、行动和自主学习的智能体，数据中心需要更多的多处理器计算能力——即结合CPU、GPU、NPU等——以支持整个智能体AI工作流程。中国已开始出现需求激增，英特尔和AMD均报告在该地区的服务器CPU出现供应短缺。同时，由于开源项目如Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw的流行，更多人开始构建本地AI智能体，导致对高端Mac Studio和Mac mini的需求也出现飙升。

AMD和英特尔谈论的主要是其CPU在数据中心的需求，消费级系统通常无法满足智能体AI的巨大内存需求。然而，如果短缺发生，且需求持续，压力可能会传导至消费市场。过去几代产品中，AMD和英特尔已将其数据中心和消费级产品线融合，通过在客户端和企业端采用相同的微架构来最大化晶圆利用率。部分芯片不会用于数据中心，因此消费市场不会消失。但如果产能像此前RAM和SSD的情况一样向数据中心倾斜，可能会对消费级供应造成压力。

与英伟达数据中心业务收入呈指数级增长不同，AMD和英特尔每季度总收入中仍有约一半来自消费市场。这仍是一个重要市场，因此尽管数据中心需求增长，但理论上不应完全以牺牲消费市场为代价。行业希望英特尔和AMD能够满足未来的需求，以避免加剧计算机行业的严峻形势。否则，若情况持续，已有人预测入门级PC可能在2028年前消失。

Get a full year of access for just $29: daily news analysis, deep dives into specialist topics in the semiconductor industry, as well as access to Bench, the largest benchmarking database around.View DealWith that out of the way, here's a sneak preview at what you can expect when subscribed. Let's take a look at a CPU comparison table to kick things off. Let's pit the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D against Intel's Core Ultra 9 285K.First, all I have to do is hit the compare tab and select the category, which spans CPUs, GPUs, Laptops, Monitors, and SSDs. Once we're in the CPU category, we can select the two products. Then you'll see a comparison between all of the data points we recorded for those two CPUs in alphabetical order.Image 1 of 3First, we select the category.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the products are selected, you can scroll through benchmarks.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)There is a wealth of data to browse through.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As well as the comparison tool, Bench offers tables of data per category. So, if you're looking to see which part performs the best in a certain workload, you can do that too.For the next example, we're going to pick out a specific GPU workload and see which consumer graphics card performs best in that scenario. Let's take the Sony hit God of War: Ragnarok. If you are building a mid-range system, then 1440p or QHD is a good spot to select for this benchmark. Since we're focused on the mid-range, let's take a look at the middle of the table. Here, we can see that the Radeon 9060 XT is faster than Nvidia's RTX 5060. These two GPUs hit around the same price point, so if I were a God of War fanatic looking to save, that would be my pick.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Toms Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)How we run BenchBench offers tons of data to browse through; these are just two examples of how you can use the tool to browse through hardware benchmarks. While the database is primarily built from the work completed while reviewing a given product, the data that you see in reviews is often truncated into geomean values, or put into a certain workload "bucket."Of course, while Bench has been live since last year, benchmarks don't stay up to date: Drivers change. New standards emerge. Therefore, we're undergoing a large-scale overhaul of our GPU and CPU tests, documenting exactly how we are benchmarking and what methodologies are being used.Recording benchmark values and taking the time to run the tests in the first place is inherently time-consuming, and Bench is the perfect reason to maintain a source of truth for all enthusiasts. What to read next Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Hands-on with the Thermal Grizzly Der8enchtable: Updating our longstanding motherboard test bench The GPU benchmarks hierarchy 2026 As for our testing methodologies, here's a glimpse of what's next for our massive database.Revamped data for GPU testing (Image credit: Future)On the GPU side of things, Senior GPU Analyst Jeffrey Kampman has the reins. He's worked out exacting testing methodologies and strategies and outlined them in a separate Tom's Hardware Premium article. For those who are not yet subscribed, we've explained why we're not using frame generation or upscaling technologies like DLSS and FSR, and how we conduct real-world benchmarks. A canned benchmark is handy, but it doesn't represent how it really feels to play a game on the hardware. And more important, it's a more accurate measure of what to expect when playing those titles for yourself.As Jeff explains, he's spent years testing GPUs in both media and dedicated game testing labs, ensuring the integrity of the results on offer. The spread of titles being tested for 2026 is also representative of what players are actually playing, not merely popular benchmarking titles. For example, we've added the single-player epic Expedition 33, in addition to the multiplayer hit Arc Raiders. Adding newer titles to the list, across a range of engines and with Ray Tracing, allows us to build a cohesive outlook on how a given GPU performs in a wide range of scenarios. (Image credit: Nvidia)Updates will be going in phases, first from current-generation hardware like Nvidia's RTX 5000 series and AMD's 9000 series, all the way back to RTX 3000 and the Radeon 6000 lineup. Of course, Intel's offerings are in the mix there too — no matter how small the market share of the dedicated GPU, it'll be being tested for 2026.The three waves of updated tests will take around a month to fully complete, but will offer you a holistic look at the GPU market, and more importantly, offer you granular testing information that you simply can't get anywhere else.A herculean CPU retesting effort (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU Analyst Jake Roach is working alongside Editor-in-Chief and former CPU reviewer Paul Alcorn to deliver a gargantuan update to our CPU tests. Their goal is building a complete dataset of CPU releases that spans over a decade of hardware. For Premium subscribers, you can read Jake's entire methodology behind CPU testing here.Between two and six machines are running concurrently, with two Golden OS images, frozen and duplicated across setups, using the same testing hardware, down to the TIM, to ensure consistent results. Of course, as we are currently testing different platforms, the motherboards used between CPUs differ, but are consistent between rigs.While we are testing hundreds of CPUs, everything from the version of the software to the processes running in the background are identical. For the CPU effort, we'll be going from AMD Excavator and 7th-gen Intel to Zen 5 and Intel Arrow Lake. That's a lot of CPUs, and even more benchmarks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Each modern CPU takes between 10 and 14 hours to test, with some older and slower models taking nearly an entire day, and with the number of chips on offer, it's going to take some time to get things up-to-date. As for the tests we run, things are split into several categories, such as applications, power/efficiency, and gaming workloads. While some tests are automated, each result is manually verified before being uploaded into our database and tools.Application testing goes across a wide range of workloads, from rendering and encoding to code compilation and data science. We're testing power efficiency across benchmarks like Y-Cruncher, Handbrake, and Prime95, just to name a few. Gaming testing is hands-on where possible. The selection of titles is broad and represents real-world players and what people are playing.As a result, no, you're not going to see something like Ashes of the Singularity, which no one plays, but is merely used as a benchmark. Instead, gaming benchmarks are selected across a range of popular titles like Monster Hunter: Wilds, DOOM: The Dark Ages, and Oblivion: Remastered, all of which use wholly different engines, and are being played by many today.Results will begin to trickle out across the database over time, and given the scale of the tests on offer, we will continue to evolve the benchmarking suite, while acknowledging that we might not revisit some of the older architectures on this list again.The benchmarks never stop (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the backdrop of a challenging year for consumer hardware, no thanks to the ongoing AI buildout and supply challenges. Getting the most out of what's available is imperative, and Bench gives you access to all of the data you need for whatever workload or game you're looking at within a handful of clicks.We've put a lot of effort into building and maintaining this feature, and we will continue to do so, documenting the process and keeping things nice and fresh for Tom's Hardware Premium subscribers.Maintaining Bench without Tom's Hardware Premium simply isn't possible. It offers us the bandwidth to test, retest, and refine our methodologies outside of a standard CPU cycle or hierarchy update. It also becomes an invaluable reference point for those of you who wish to seek out specific hardware workloads, going beyond our standard reviews and wading through the data yourself.We're never going to stop testing, and Bench is the perfect place to compile our data. As we look forward to the next generation of PC hardware, we'll be sure to keep things up-to-date, giving you access to the cold, hard data behind our testing. In addition to our testing updates, we will also keep working behind the scenes to add new functionality to Bench in the coming months.Tom's Hardware Premium is priced at $3 a month when taking out an annual plan, or $7 for a rolling monthly subscription. Bench is just a single piece of what's on offer. To take advantage of the ultimate resource for enthusiasts, be sure to check it out today.
items.author: The Editors of Tom's Hardware
items.titleZh: 依托Bench超越评测，互联网上极尽深入的消费级硬件基准数据库——横跨多品类比较数百款产品。
items.descriptionZh:
近日，知名硬件媒体Tom's Hardware宣布，其付费订阅服务Tom's Hardware Premium的核心功能——Bench基准测试数据库——正在进行大规模更新和扩展，旨在为硬件爱好者提供更全面、更精确的性能对比工具。这项服务以每年29美元或每月3美元的价格提供，包含独家新闻分析、硬件路线图和庞大的基准测试数据库。

**背景与核心功能**
Bench是Tom's Hardware Premium的独家性能分析工具，它聚合了该网站在评测各类硬件产品时收集的海量真实世界和合成基准测试数据。用户可以通过直观的界面，轻松对比任意两款同类产品（如CPU、GPU、笔记本电脑、显示器和SSD）的性能数据，所有结果以清晰的表格形式呈现，并按字母顺序排列。

**使用演示与数据价值**
1.  **CPU对比**：例如，用户可以快速对比AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D与Intel Core Ultra 9 285K这两款旗舰处理器在数十项测试中的表现。工具直接展示所有已记录的数据点，方便进行全方位评估。
2.  **GPU场景化筛选**：除了直接对比，用户还可以按特定工作负载筛选数据。例如，可以选择在游戏《战神：诸神黄昏》的1440p分辨率下，查看所有显卡的性能排名。数据显示，在相近价位段，Radeon 9060 XT的表现领先于NVIDIA RTX 5060，为玩家提供了具体的选购参考。

**技术原理与创新：大规模重测与方法论革新**
Bench数据库的独特价值在于其数据的深度和一致性。尽管数据库已运行一年，但为了应对驱动更新、新标准出现和游戏迭代，Tom's Hardware正在对其GPU和CPU测试体系进行“海格力斯式”的全面革新。

*   **GPU测试革新**：由高级GPU分析师Jeffrey Kampman主导。新方法论**摒弃了帧生成（如DLSS3/FSR3）和超分辨率技术（如DLSS/FSR）**，专注于反映真实游戏体验的“手动实测”数据。测试游戏阵容基于玩家实际游玩的热门作品（如新增的《远征33》、《Arc Raiders》），覆盖不同引擎和光线追踪场景，而非过时、仅用于跑分的游戏。更新将分三波进行，涵盖从当前一代RTX 5000/Radeon 9000系列上溯至RTX 3000/Radeon 6000系列以及Intel显卡的所有产品，历时约一个月，旨在提供市场全景图和无法在其他地方获得的细粒度数据。
*   **CPU测试革新**：由CPU分析师Jake Roach和前CPU评测主编Paul Alcorn联手，目标构建一个**跨越十年以上、涵盖从AMD挖掘机架构/Intel第七代酷睿到最新Zen 5/Arrow Lake的完整CPU数据集**。他们建立了高度一致的测试环境：使用2至6台机器并行测试，采用相同的“黄金系统镜像”、散热硅脂（TIM）和外围硬件，确保结果可比性。每颗现代CPU需10-14小时完成测试，老旧型号可能耗时近一天。测试类别包括：
    *   **应用测试**：覆盖渲染、编码、代码编译、数据科学等。
    *   **能效测试**：使用Y-Cruncher、Handbrake、Prime95等。
    *   **游戏测试**：选择《怪物猎人：荒野》、《毁灭战士：黑暗时代》、《上古卷轴4：湮没重制版》等当前热门且引擎多样的游戏，取代无人游玩但常被用于跑分的软件。

**性能参数与工作负载**
测试过程极其严谨，软件版本、后台进程均严格统一。所有结果在上传数据库前都经过人工验证。由于规模庞大，此次全面重测结果将逐步更新至数据库，且团队坦承，部分古老架构未来可能不会再次重测。

**行业影响与应用场景**
在当前消费级硬件面临AI热潮导致的供应链挑战和成本压力的背景下，帮助用户最大化利用现有资源显得尤为重要。Bench数据库使用户能在几次点击内，获取针对特定工作负载或游戏的最详尽性能数据，超越了常规评测文章的概括性结论。

**服务可持续性与未来**
Tom's Hardware强调，维持Bench这样深度和广度的数据库，离不开Premium订阅服务的支持。这使得团队有资源在常规评测周期之外，持续进行重测和方法论优化。Bench不仅是数据的仓库，更将成为硬件爱好者深入研究、自主分析不可或缺的参考工具。团队承诺将持续测试、更新数据，并计划在未来数月为Bench添加新功能。

总之，Tom's Hardware通过Bench数据库及其持续的大规模更新，致力于为硬件社区建立一个权威、透明且深度可定制化的性能“真相之源”，帮助用户在复杂的硬件市场中做出明智决策。

history.title: US Commerce Department confirms harsh new AI export rules, shoots down reports over the return of Biden-era AI Diffusion rule — DoC to formalize a new approach to strategic AI accelerator export controls
history.description: The U.S. Commerce confirms work on new export regulations for AI accelerators, but implies they will not resemble the ill-fated AI Diffusion Rule.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. Department of Commerce has issued a statement confirming that the U.S. government is looking to change AI hardware export rules, but provided little by way of detail. The officials confirmed that the U.S. DoC is looking to formalize an approach under which buyers of large quantities of AI accelerators must invest in U.S. AI infrastructure to obtain their hardware.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the region"The Commerce Department is committed to promoting secure exports of the American tech stack," a statement by the U.S. DoC reads. "We successfully advanced exports through our historic Middle East agreements, and there are ongoing internal government discussions about formalizing that approach."The newly proposed export rules introduce a multi-level licensing structure tied to computing capacity. Smaller shipments of up to 1,000 Nvidia GB300 GPUs would undergo an expedited approval process; medium-scale deployments would need to secure pre-authorization from the U.S. Department of Commerce before applying for export license as well as operational transparency, disclosure of business activities, and potential on-site inspections by U.S. authorities; while planned large AI clusters set to use 200,000 of GB300 GPUs or more and operated by one entity in one country would require commitments to invest in U.S. AI infrastructure as part of the arrangement as well as intergovernmental talks with the U.S. to make national security assurances. You may like U.S. government preps sweeping export controls for Nvidia, AMD AI hardware Analyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rules U.S. posts official H200 and MI325X AI GPU export rules to China, but with plenty of caveats The terms attached to recent export licenses allowing Cerebras and Nvidia to supply hardware to the United Arab Emirates included a requirement that the Middle Eastern country invest one dollar in U.S. AI infrastructure for every dollar spent on its own domestic AI buildouts. If the same terms were to be attached to export licenses to other countries, then this would make hardware from AMD, Cerebras, Nvidia, and other suppliers of AI accelerators 2X more expensive for companies in these countries.The U.S. DoC shot down claims of a return to anything resembling the AI Diffusion Rule from the Joe Biden era: "Today there was reporting that we were returning to the AI diffusion rule," the statement reads. "We will not. It was burdensome, overreaching, and disastrous."While the AI Diffusion Rule was indeed very complicated, some approaches of the new rumored regulations would make them even more burdensome than the criticized export regime from early 2025.The AI Diffusion Rule divided countries into three tiers based on trust and risk levels. Tier 1 included the U.S. and 18 close allies like Australia, Canada, Japan, Taiwan, the U.K., and much of Western Europe, which faced minimal restrictions on AI exports. In Tier 2 countries, entities could import up to 1,700 Nvidia H100 GPUs (or equivalent) without a license, and these did not count toward national AI chip limits. Tier 3 was banned altogether.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMeanwhile, getting up to 320,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs required entities in Tier 2 countries to qualify as National Validated End Users (NVEUs) and meet strict security requirements. Unverified organizations in Tier 2 were allowed to buy up to 50,000 GPUs per country, though the limit was expandable to 100,000 if their government reached a formal agreement with the U.S. Under no circumstances did the AI Diffusion Rule mandate investments in U.S. AI infrastructure by purchasers of American AI hardware.If the new regulation is enacted, anything above 1,000 GB300 would be subject to pre-authorization before export licenses could be issued, which includes shipments to 18 allied nations. Getting 200,000 or more GB300 GPUs would essentially mandate investments in the U.S. AI buildouts. Then again, the reported rules are not final, and many of the requirements may be removed in the final versions of the proposed export regime. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: 美国商务部确认严苛人工智能出口新规，驳斥拜登时期模型规则重启传闻——将明确战略人工智能加速器出口管控新方案
history.descriptionZh:
美国商务部近期证实正在酝酿修改人工智能硬件出口管制规则，其核心方向是要求大规模采购美国AI加速器的买家必须投资美国本土AI基础设施，以此作为获取硬件的条件。尽管具体实施细则尚未公布，但已披露的框架草案显示出比此前“AI扩散规则”更为复杂和严格的管控思路。

**新规核心机制：分级许可与投资挂钩**
根据披露的草案，新规将建立与算力规模直接挂钩的多级许可制度：
1.  **小规模采购（≤1,000张Nvidia GB300 GPU）**：适用快速审批流程。
2.  **中等规模采购**：需在申请出口许可证前获得美国商务部的预先授权，同时采购方需承诺运营透明度、披露商业活动，并接受美方的潜在现场检查。
3.  **大规模集群采购（≥200,000张GB300 GPU，且由单一实体在同一国家运营）**：这将成为管控重点。采购方不仅需满足上述所有条件，还必须承诺投资美国AI基础设施，并需要通过政府间对话向美国提供国家安全保证。

**关键创新与潜在成本影响**
本次规则修订最引人注目的创新点在于引入了 **“投资对等”原则**。此前美国向阿拉伯联合酋长国发放的出口许可中已出现类似条款，要求阿联酋在美投资1美元AI基础设施，才能为其国内AI建设花费1美元购买美国硬件。若此原则推广至其他国家，意味着采购美国AI加速器（涵盖AMD、Cerebras、Nvidia等供应商）的实际成本将可能**翻倍**。

**与旧规“AI扩散规则”的对比分析**
美国商务部明确否认新规是回归2025年初实施但备受批评的“AI扩散规则”，并称旧规则“繁琐、越权且具有破坏性”。两者核心差异如下：

*   **旧规（AI扩散规则）**：基于国家信任度分为三级。
    *   **第一级**：美国及18个亲密盟友（如澳、加、日、英及西欧多国），几乎无限制。
    *   **第二级**：实体可无证进口最多1,700张H100（或等效算力），不计入国家总额度。要获取多达32万张H100，需成为“国家验证最终用户”并满足严格安全要求。未经验证的组织每国最多可采购5万张（经政府间协议可扩至10万张）。**该规则从未强制要求采购方投资美国基础设施。**

*   **新规（草案）**：
    *   **适用范围扩大**：即便对第一级盟友，超过1,000张GB300的出货也需预先授权。
    *   **新增捆绑条件**：采购规模达到20万张GB300或以上时，强制要求投资美国AI基础设施。
    *   **流程更复杂**：增加了预授权、运营透明度审查及政府间安全保证谈判等多重环节。

**技术影响与应用场景展望**
若新规落地，将对全球AI产业格局产生深远影响：
1.  **供应链成本与结构**：追求超大规模AI集群（如用于训练前沿大模型）的国家或企业，将面临硬件采购成本显著上升和供应链复杂性增加的双重压力。这可能促使他们调整采购策略，或加速本土及非美替代供应链的发展。
2.  **地缘技术竞争**：规则将商业采购与地缘政治承诺（国家安全保证、基础设施投资）深度捆绑，使AI硬件贸易进一步政治化。这可能会加剧全球AI技术生态的分化。
3.  **市场准入**：对于Nvidia等美国厂商而言，其在关键区域（如文中提及的中国市场）的市场份额战略，将不得不更加复杂地平衡商业利益与合规要求，以维持其生态（如CUDA）的全球影响力。

目前，该规则仍处于讨论阶段，最终版本可能删减部分要求。但其清晰的导向表明，美国正试图通过出口管制工具，不仅限制竞争对手获取先进算力，更意图直接吸引全球资源回流，以巩固其自身在AI基础设施领域的领先地位。

history.title: Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition review:  Silent running
history.description: Asus’ GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is built with a single-minded focus on quiet operation. It takes the world’s second-fastest gaming GPU and reduces its noise levels to the absolute minimum thanks to an enormous heatsink and three cutting-edge Noctua fans. But its size, weight, high price, and polarizing design all make it a product for the Noctua faithful and quiet computing obsessives only.
history.content: Asus and Noctua have been collaborating on ultra-quiet graphics cards for some time now by pairing Noctua’s most advanced 120mm fans with massive custom heatsinks. That collaboration has continued in the Blackwell generation with the Asus RTX 5080 Noctua Edition, an absolutely ginormous air-cooled graphics card that promises no-compromises performance and temperatures alongside the lowest possible noise levels. That’s an exceedingly high bar to clear in one product. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Making air-cooled computer hardware quieter is simple enough, in theory. Improve thermal transfer by adding a vapor chamber or heat pipes to the base plate of a heatsink, increase its surface area by adding more and larger fins to the fin stack, and take advantage of the improved heat dissipation by slowing down the fans cooling said heatsink. Eventually, you get imperceptible noise levels. Easy enough, right?In practice, this recipe runs into all sorts of obstacles. Cases can only accept so large a heatsink without running into clearance issues. Sockets, slots, and PCBs can only take so much weight before they start to deform. The heatsink itself can only be so costly as part of the overall bill of materials. If you’re an engineer designing a typical graphics card for the typical PC, you have to balance all these concerns, and louder, faster-spinning fans on a smaller heatsink are typically one result of those tradeoffs.The Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition embraces an entirely different set of constraints. This graphics card is all about low noise levels, and the two companies have spared no effort or expense in making the quietest possible GPU air cooler out there, size and weight be damned.We’ve had the pleasure of reviewing some of Asus’s Noctua Edition cards in the past, and they’ve certainly provided both impressive noise levels and thermal performance. But this RTX 5080 marks the first time the duo has deployed three such fans on a Noctua Edition graphics card: in this case, NF-A12x25 G2s. As someone who got his start as a case and heatsink reviewer many moons ago, those fans immediately stand out as something different. $1,699.99 at ASUS $2,072.99 at Amazon Check Walmart (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The NF-A12x25 G2s boast a list of engineering refinements that would make an aeronautical engineer blush. Everything from the curvature of each blade to the ridges on the fan hub to the winglets at each blade tip is said to be optimized to improve the distribution and evenness of airflow with typical Noctua obsessiveness. The impellers (or rotors) themselves sit so close to the fan frame that trying to slide a sheet of printer paper between them will make the impeller move. Crazy stuff.To further refine the noise character of the card, Noctua has supplied Asus with two types of NF-A12x25 G2 fans, one of which runs slightly slower and the other of which runs slightly faster than the other. Noctua says this avoids “periodic humming or vibrations caused by beat frequencies.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The fans are paired with a custom heatsink that pairs a vapor-chamber baseplate with 11 heat pipes running through a 14.5” long fin stack, which is certainly plenty of metal. But not all of the card’s four-slot height is dedicated to fins and heat pipes. About two slots are occupied by the full-size NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We didn’t take our Noctua Edition apart because it’s a loaner, but we can see that the huge vapor chamber at the base of the heatsink covers both the GPU itself and the GDDR7 memory that rings it for a complete thermal solution. The VRM power phases and inductors are also joined to the fin stack with their own metal contact plate and thermal pads. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this is one of the largest and heaviest air-cooled graphics cards I’ve ever handled, weighing in at a whopping 5.9 lb (2.7 kg). It absolutely dwarfs the RTX 5080 Founders Edition. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)For all its focus on quiet operation, the design of the RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is inescapably polarizing. The plastic fan shroud and metal backplate are all finished in a muted brown color with a subtle sparkle that only reveals itself under direct lighting. The NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves are the brown-and-tan models that Noctua obsessives will love and the uninitiated may hate. I think it’s great, but other Tom’s Hardware staffers can’t stand it. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Noctua faithful will find plenty of subtle nods to its brand on this 5080, and these touches are all done with the same subtlety and attention to detail typical of other Noctua products. Metallic accents on the face of the card suggest wings or eyebrows, and the flow-through cutout on the backplate borrows one half of Noctua’s owl logo. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The painted stripes on the backplate that suggest stampings or embossing are so well done that they made me do a double-take to ensure they weren’t actually part of the metal. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this card will look fan-tastic in a Noctua-themed build, but many will be left wishing for a Chromax model that’s dressed entirely in black for better coordination in the average PC. Maybe a future Noctua Edition can cover both bases, but for now, brown is all you get.Let's take a look at performance, power, and thermals on the following pages.SpecsSwipe to scroll horizontallyHeader Cell - Column 0 RTX 5080 Founders EditionAsus RTX 5080 Noctua EditionGPUNvidia GB203Nvidia GB203SMs8484CUDA Cores1075210752Boost Clock (MHz)26172700VRAM TypeGDDR7GDDR7VRAM Clock17501750VRAM Capacity (GB)1616Memory Bandwidth (GB/s)960960Peak FP32 TFLOPS (Boost)56.358.1TGP (Watts)360360Power connectors1x 12V-2x61x 12V-2x6Recommended PSU (Watts)850850Dimensions (LWD)12" x 4.8" x 1.6" (30.5 x 12.2 x 4 cm)15" x 5.3" x 3.2" (38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1 cm)Weight3.6 lb (1.6 kg)5.9 lb (2.67 kg)MSRP$999.99$1,699.99MORE: Best Graphics CardsMORE: GPU Benchmarks and HierarchyMORE: All Graphics Content
history.author: Jeffrey Kampman
history.titleZh: 华硕RTX 5080猫头鹰版评测：静默运行
history.descriptionZh:
华硕（Asus）与知名散热器制造商猫头鹰（Noctua）长期合作，致力于打造极致静音的风冷显卡。双方将猫头鹰顶级的120mm风扇与特制巨型散热器结合，此前已推出多款产品。如今，这一合作延续至Blackwell世代，推出了华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition。这款风冷显卡以庞大的体积为代价，旨在实现毫不妥协的性能与温度控制，并追求可能的最低噪音水平，这无疑是一个极高的设计目标。

从理论上讲，降低风冷硬件噪音的原理相对直接：通过改进散热底座（如采用均热板或增加热管）来提升热传导效率；通过增大散热鳍片的规模和面积来增加散热表面积；进而利用更优异的散热能力来降低风扇转速，最终达到难以察觉的噪音水平。然而，在实际产品设计中，这一方案面临诸多限制：机箱兼容性、主板插槽与PCB的承重能力、以及整体物料成本。因此，常规显卡设计必须在这些因素间取得平衡，其结果往往是采用体积较小的散热器并搭配转速更高、噪音更大的风扇。

华硕GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition则完全跳出了上述常规约束。这款显卡的唯一核心目标就是低噪音，两家公司不惜一切代价和努力，致力于打造目前可能最安静的风冷GPU散热方案，而将尺寸和重量问题置于次要地位。

此次推出的RTX 5080版本标志着双方首次在猫头鹰版显卡上部署了三风扇设计，具体型号为三把NF-A12x25 G2风扇。这款风扇凝聚了猫头鹰标志性的极致工程优化，其改进清单足以令航空工程师惊叹。从每个扇叶的曲率、扇毂上的导流沟槽，到叶尖的小翼设计，一切都旨在以典型的猫头鹰式执着优化气流分布与均匀性。其转子与扇框的间隙极小，甚至难以插入一张普通打印纸，这有助于减少湍流和噪音。

为进一步优化噪音特征，猫头鹰为华硕提供了两种略有差异的NF-A12x25 G2风扇，其转速一快一慢。猫头鹰表示，这种设计可以避免因频率共振而产生的“周期性嗡嗡声或振动”。

这些风扇与一个特制散热器相连。该散热器采用均热板底座，并贯穿了11根热管，热管穿过一个长达14.5英寸（约36.8厘米）的鳍片阵列。然而，这款占据四槽位高度的显卡，并非所有空间都用于鳍片和热管，其中大约两槽的厚度被全尺寸的NF-A12x25 G2风扇本身所占据。散热器底部的巨型均热板完整覆盖了GPU核心及环绕其周围的GDDR7显存，实现了完整的核心散热。VRM供电模块和电感也通过独立的金属接触板及导热垫与鳍片阵列相连。

最终成果是史上最大、最重的风冷显卡之一，重量高达5.9磅（约2.7公斤），其体积远远超过NVIDIA的RTX 5080 Founders Edition公版卡。

尽管专注于静音，但RTX 5080 Noctua Edition的设计注定引发两极评价。其塑料风扇罩和金属背板均采用哑光棕色配色，仅在直射光下呈现细微闪亮效果。NF-A12x25 G2风扇本身也是经典的棕褐色款式，猫头鹰忠实拥趸会热爱此设计，但未接触过的用户可能难以接受。显卡正面装饰有暗示翅膀或眉毛的金属点缀，背板的通风开孔借鉴了猫头鹰品牌猫头鹰Logo的一半元素。背板上的喷绘条纹模仿冲压或浮雕效果，工艺精湛。总的来说，这款显卡在猫头鹰主题的装机中会显得极为出彩，但许多用户可能更期待未来能推出全黑色的Chromax版本以更好地搭配主流PC。

**性能规格对比与分析：**
根据提供的规格表，华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition在核心规格（如SM数量、CUDA核心数、显存类型、容量、带宽及TGP功耗）上与公版RTX 5080 Founders Edition完全一致。其主要提升和差异体现在：
1.  **频率与性能**：其Boost时钟频率从公版的2617MHz提升至2700MHz，这使得其峰值FP32算力（Boost）达到58.1 TFLOPS，略高于公版的56.3 TFLOPS。
2.  **尺寸与重量**：这是最显著的差异。其尺寸（38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1 厘米）远大于公版（30.5 x 12.2 x 4 厘米），重量（2.67公斤）更是比公版（1.6公斤）重了约67%。
3.  **价格**：其建议零售价高达1699.99美元，相比公版卡的999.99美元有大幅溢价。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
华硕RTX 5080 Noctua Edition代表了风冷显卡设计的一个极端方向。它证明了通过不计体积和成本的极致堆料，确实能在不牺牲性能的前提下，将高功耗GPU的噪音控制在极低水平。这为那些对噪音极其敏感的用户（如音频制作、静音办公环境、追求极致安静体验的发烧友）提供了顶级风冷解决方案。它重新定义了“旗舰风冷”的标准，即不再仅仅追求绝对散热效能，而是将“静音下的效能”作为更高维度的目标。然而，其庞大的体积、惊人的重量和昂贵的售价也决定了它只能是面向小众高端市场的产品，对机箱兼容性和主板承重提出了严峻挑战。它更像是一个展示双方工程理念和技术实力的“光环产品”，其设计思路可能会影响未来高端风冷显卡在平衡噪音与效能时对散热模组规模的重新考量。

history.title: Arc Raiders was accidentally recording Discord conversations into an unencrypted local game file — vulnerability in SDK could log messages and credentials in plaintext
history.description: A vulnerability in Discord's SDK allowed Arc Raiders to unintentionally log private DMs and user account credentials to the game's log files. Embark Studios has since hotfixed the problem.
